gaps.after: , the researchers refused to abandon their idea.
gaps.explanation: 「三度失敗した → それでもあきらめなかった」という逆接。nevertheless（それにもかかわらず）が前後の対立をつなぐ。therefore は因果で不自然。

gaps.before: Not until she read the final page
gaps.after: how the whole story fit together.
gaps.explanation: 否定語句 Not until ... が文頭に出ると倒置が起き、主節は疑問文の語順（did + 主語 + 動詞の原形）になる。よって did she realize が正しい。

gaps.after: on the theory that everyone had accepted for years.
gaps.explanation: cast doubt on ~ で「～に疑いを投げかける」という定番のコロケーション。cast と自然に結びつく名詞は doubt。

readingQuestions.prompt: 文脈に最も合う選択肢を選んでください。

gaps.after: here since 1920 and still carries heavy traffic every day.
gaps.explanation: since 1920（過去のある時点から現在まで）が続く継続の意味なので現在完了 has stood が正しい。過去形 stood では現在までの継続を表せない。

gaps.after: , she still offered to help us carry the boxes upstairs.
gaps.explanation: 「疲れ果てていた → それでも手伝うと申し出た」という譲歩・逆接。nonetheless（それでもなお）が適切。because や so は因果関係で不自然。

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Return of the Wolves
paragraphs: When the last wolves were killed in Yellowstone National Park in the 1920s, few people mourned them. To the farmers and hunters of the time, the animals were simply dangerous pests. For decades the park seemed calmer without them. Yet beneath the surface something was quietly going wrong: with no predators left to fear, the deer multiplied and grazed the young trees along the rivers down to bare stubble.

In 1995, scientists made a bold decision and brought a small number of wolves back. The results surprised almost everyone. As the deer learned to avoid the open valleys where they could easily be caught, the willows and aspens they had once stripped began to grow tall again. Birds returned to the fresh branches, beavers built dams in the recovering streams, and those dams created quiet ponds for fish and frogs.

Most remarkable of all, the rivers themselves changed. With strong new roots holding the banks in place, the water ran clearer and followed steadier paths. A single species, returned to its old home, had reshaped an entire landscape. The story of the wolves reminds us that in nature the pieces are connected in ways we are only beginning to understand.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Bringing back a single predator can transform an entire ecosystem in unexpected ways.
Wolves are dangerous pests that should be kept out of national parks.
Deer are the most important animals in Yellowstone National Park.
Rivers change their paths only because of rainfall and weather.

questions.before: After the wolves were removed, the deer multiplied because they had no predators left to

questions.prompt: オオカミが戻った後に川そのものがどう変化したかを述べている語句はどれですか。

spans.text: the deer multiplied and grazed the young trees along the rivers down to bare stubble
the willows and aspens they had once stripped began to grow tall again
the water ran clearer and followed steadier paths

questions.sentence: Ecologists call this chain of connected effects a trophic cascade.

questions.prompt: 筆者が本文全体で最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: One returning species reshaped an entire park, showing how deeply connected the natural world is.
Killing predators is the best way to keep a national park safe and calm.
The wolves of Yellowstone had no real effect on the plants or rivers.
Scientists should never move animals from one place to another.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Farms on the Rooftops
paragraphs: High above the noise and traffic of many modern cities, a quiet change is taking place on the roofs of buildings. Where there was once nothing but grey concrete and old pipes, people are now growing vegetables, herbs, and even small fruit trees. These rooftop gardens turn wasted space into something useful and green.

The benefits reach far beyond fresh food. Plants on a roof soak up rain that would otherwise flood the streets below, and they help cool the building in summer, cutting the need for air conditioning. For the people who tend them, the gardens also offer a rare chance to slow down, dig in the soil, and get to know their neighbours.

Of course, rooftop farming is not always easy. The wind can be fierce, water must be carried up high, and not every roof is strong enough to hold the heavy weight of wet soil. Even so, as cities grow more and more crowded, many people believe that the empty spaces above our heads may become some of the most valuable land of all.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Rooftop gardens turn unused city space into a source of food and other benefits.
Cities should remove all plants from the tops of their buildings.
Growing food is only possible on farms far outside the city.
Air conditioning is the best way to cool a building in summer.

questions.before: The writer says that plants on a roof soak up rain that would otherwise

questions.prompt: 屋上農園の難しさ（デメリット）を述べている語句はどれですか。

spans.text: people are now growing vegetables, herbs, and even small fruit trees
the gardens also offer a rare chance to slow down
The wind can be fierce, water must be carried up high
they help cool the building in summer

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Although rooftop farming has real challenges, it makes crowded cities greener and more useful.
Rooftop farming is so difficult that no one should ever attempt it.
The only reason to build a rooftop garden is to grow fruit trees.
Cities with tall buildings can never find space to grow any plants.
